Common name of taxon
TRUE=Representative Important Species (with length measurements that are used for age class metrics)
number of possible age classes (RIS taxa only)
TRUE = present at Lower Boise River mainstem sites used to calibrate the Lower Boise River BCG model (version 1)
taxonomic hierarchy after Cyprinidae was broken up into LEUCISCIDAE and CYPRINIDAE
CYPRINIDAE = used to be in the CYPRINIDAE family
taxonomic hierarchy
TRUE = hybrid (offspring of two different fish species)
FALSE = target (ok to include in the BCG calculations). TRUE = nontarget (not appropriate to include in the BCG calculation)
Specifies whether a taxon is native or non-native to the Lower Boise River HUC12 basin. Entries = NATIVE or NON_NATIVE

Biological Condition Gradient (BCG) taxa attribute assignments for the Lower Boise River BCG: 2 = highly sensitive; 3 = sensitive ubiquitous; 4 = moderately tolerant; 5 = highly tolerant; 6=non-native
BCG attribute IV sub-groups: 4_better; 4_middle; 4_worse
BCG taxa attribute in Roman numeral format
Habitat type: benthic (B); water-column (W)
Trophic type: Herbivore (HB); Invertivore (IV); Omnivore (OM); top-carnivore (TC). A single taxon can have multiple entries.
Thermal preference assignment: cold; cool; warm
Reproduction type: only populated with LITHOPHIL for Lower Boise BCG. Can add more later (BEARER; BROADCASTER; COMPLEX NEST; MIGRATORY; SIMPLE NEST). A single taxon can have multiple entries.
TRUE = need connectivity to complete life cycle
yes = occurred in the Lower Boise mainstem BCG calibration dataset
